video_title: A #DDD Desert Road Trip with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: A Sec
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Arroyo Seco, New Mexico, which is right between Taos and Taoski Valley. This little town's got this cool restaurant. It's been here for about 10 years, and it's had three owners. The most recent owner actually started in the restaurant as a line cook. Oh, he's got a big imagination, a great palate, and an exciting menu, and I hear he's a member of Flavortown. This is a sec.

dishes.description: Poutine-esque fries topped with braised bison short ribs and red chili gravy, cheese curds, garnished with parsley.
dishes.guy_response: That's dangerous. I'm a big shoestring guy. I want the crunch. The braise on the short rib is delicious. And the shreds great. The cheese curd is stretchy and squeaky. Rich, roasted. Smoky. Oh, no. I love it. Cheese curding on bison chili fry. The cheese curds are great. The red chili spot on. The bison was very moist, was shredding. I've eaten a lot of fries in my life, and these are the absolute best fries I've ever had.

restaurants.name: Nexus Brewery and Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: So here I am about 10 minutes north of downtown Albuquerque, New Mexico, right off the I-25, in a big industrial area that's packed with... Wait for it. No, wait for it. Exactly what you're thinking. Micro breweries. A bunch of them. But we're here to see one in particular. This joint. Nexus Brewery and Restaurant.

dishes.description: Chicken brined for 24 hours, double-dredged in seasoned flour, fried, served with collard greens, mashed potatoes, and gravy.
dishes.guy_response: Man, there's a ton of crunch. But it's not... The brine's not... The brine's not too heavy. And even though you had all that hot sauce in there, it's not spicy. It's just really flavorful. Nice mashed potatoes. Good. Crunchy chicken. A lot of flavor. And my favorite out of all of them is the greens, which I can't believe I'm finding collard greens in New Mexico. That's mandatory right there.

restaurants.name: Perk Eatery
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm cruising through Scottsdale, Arizona to check out a joint where, get this, the wife is a second-generation Albanian. The husband is an immigrant from Mexico. And together, they get this breakfast and lunch joint where they're scrambling... And the cops make it about 95% of the menu. This is Perk Eatery.

dishes.description: Tortillas filled with chipotle ricotta, zesty black beans, slow-roasted pork, topped with spicy New Mexico chili sauce, two eggs, and fresh cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: This is really good. The New Mexican chili enchilada sauce is right on point. A little bit of heat. Nice little sweetness to it from that honey. Call it a breakfast enchilada. Call it a breakfast burrito. Call it whatever you want. Just don't call me late for it.

restaurants.name: The Fry Bread House
restaurants.guy_intro: Here in Phoenix, Arizona, love this town for Triple D. Been here a ton of times. Great chefs, great restaurants, all kind of diversity. Here to check out a restaurant that won a James Beard award back in 2012. And you know what? We need to see more of this food and more of this culture. Native American. You're going to dig it. This is the Fry Bread House.

dishes.description: Pork loin seared and added to a stew with hominy, onions, garlic, oregano, black pepper, cayenne, red chili sauce, cilantro, green onions. Served with chumath (tortilla-like bread).
dishes.guy_response: I like that. I like this scarring. That's good pozole, tender pork, nice and light, not shy on flavor at all. Good. Delicious. I love it. I'm working on the pozole. The pozole here is unique. It's a thicker stew. It has such a great flavor. The spice makes it amazing.

restaurants.name: Chula Artisan Eatery
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in La Quinta, which is about 30 minutes from Palm Springs, California, and I'm looking for this place. I got to tell you something, it was not easy to find, but I hear it's well worth it because if you're looking for homemade, scratch-made, real deal Mexican with a twist, they tell me this is the place Chula Artisan Eatery.

dishes.dish_name: Mom's Red Chili Chilaquiles with Pulled Pork
dishes.description: House-made corn chips topped with red chile sauce, pulled pork, green onion, queso fresco, avocado, microgreens, chile oil, and onion jam.
dishes.guy_response: The chile sauce, it's silky, a lot of chile flavor. You do this really dynamite pork, you go a little bit extra with that. The chips are nice and crunchy, the jam is just that kind of that funky little twist that makes it yours. That's the next level. Dynamite.

restaurants.name: Gorilla Burger
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Palm Desert, California, just about 20 minutes from Palm Springs. I'm here to check out a joint where a family of four is serving up some classic and not so classic burgers. And when I was there in 2017, I got my hands on a few. Cut to now, and the family's still there. The burgers are still there. And the crowds are coming in hot. This is Gorilla Burger.

dishes.description: Burger patty with chipotle onions and peppers, chipotle ketchup, pepper jack cheese, bacon, sriracha mayo, and habanero salsa.
dishes.guy_response: You do all these little things that add to it, so you get this nice, really well-rounded heat. Mm. Oh, dynamite burger. Nice job, chef.

restaurants.name: El Marisol
restaurants.guy_intro: Here to check out another joint in Palm Springs. Let's see how's the story go. A guy from Guadalajara wants to have a better life, moves to the United States, gets here, finds a restaurant, buys it, gets married, has kids, brings family recipes, and he's been doing it for 37 years. That's the real deal. And this is El Marisol.

dishes.description: Chicken breast cooked in a broth, then shredded and served with a pipián sauce made from toasted guajillo, chile de arbol, california chiles, pumpkin seeds, and blue corn. Served with Mexican rice and beans.
dishes.guy_response: The freshness of the tortilla. Is really good flavor. The chicken is tender. The Diabla. Hello. The sauce is hot. And the pipián. It's got the flavor of the enchilada. But the richness of the mole. Outstanding. The rice is nice and light and flaky. The beans are creamy and silky. That's got to be something you could serve to your mom. I mean, legit. Pollo pipián right here. Pollo pipián tastes a little spicy. But really good. Their chicken is outstanding.

restaurants.name: Emma Jean's Holland Burger Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here on the California side of Route 66 just outside of Victorville, California. It is so hot. There are tons of trucks. And I am hungry. So now it's time to play the where am I going to stop and eat game. So, with my keen sense of awareness, I'm going to stop and eat where the truckers eat. Emma Jean's Hall & Burger Cafe.

dishes.description: Scratch-made biscuits with a thick sausage gravy.
dishes.guy_response: That's a real deal biscuit right there. That's tasty. I don't think I could eat more than six or 12 of these.

restaurants.name: Pyrenees Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: Boy, do I have one for you. A joint from 1901. So here I am on the east side of Bakersfield, California, right by the Southern Pacific Railroad. Now from 1901 to 1935, this place was a lot of different joints. Since 1935, everybody in Bakersfield knows this place. This is the Pyrenees Cafe.

dishes.description: Chicken seasoned with salt, pepper, garlic powder, chicken base, fresh garlic, olive oil, marinated for at least two hours, then soaked in buttermilk, dredged in seasoned flour, and pan-fried. Served with green beans and french fries, topped with a garlic sauce.
dishes.guy_response: The garlic on the outside. It's very strong. But the garlic and the flavor inside the chicken is what is really nice. It's tender. It's juicy. Lots of flavor. The crust on the chicken is great. I have restaurants called Johnny Garlic's. I have one in Bakersfield. But if I was going to ever make fried garlic chicken, that would be the example. Outstanding. The garlic just hits you right away as soon as you bite into it. Probably don't want to have your first date after you eat it, but it's very, very good. The best. It's the best chicken that you'll ever have.

restaurants.name: Full Belly Deli
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm just like you guys. I mean, not just for Triple D, but for me and for my family. I'm always looking for great restaurants. I'm always on the hunt. So a few years ago, I was in Truckee, California. Everybody tells me about this place. You got to get the sandwich here. So I did, and it was dynamite. Well, now I'm in Reno, Nevada, and I find out these dudes have got a second location. I got to check it out. This is Full Belly Deli.

dishes.description: Burrito filled with chorizo sausage gravy, scratch-made biscuits, hash browns, and cheddar cheese, then pressed on a panini press.
dishes.guy_response: Well, I'm going to tell you, first thing, as scared of it as I was. For some reason, the way you've made it, the way you've seasoned it, it really is not overly heavy. It's delicious. The biscuit is light and flaky in there. The crunch of the hash brown inside is really nice, but the kicker for me is the gravy, bro. And I just have a bowl of gravy. This is a ski bum delight, 101. Well done, man. Yeah, thank you very much. Well done.
